do: Honor the Æsir and Vanir daily through prayer, offerings, and rituals.
Perform blot (sacrificial rites) at appropriate times of year and life events.
Observe sacred days such as solstices, equinoxes, and local holy days.
Use runes for divination, protection, and blessing only with knowledge and respect.
Maintain a hof (temple) or sacred space clean and consecrated.
Seek wisdom from seiðr, prophecy, and dreams, but act with discretion.
Offer thanks to the landvættir (local spirits) when entering or leaving new places.
do_not: Do not mock or neglect the Gods, Goddesses, ancestors, or spirits.
Do not practice magic for purely selfish gain or harm others unjustly.
Do not defile sacred sites, shrines, or burial mounds.
Do not ignore omens, signs, or runic messages, believing them trivial.
Do not perform rituals under the influence of uncontrolled rage or malice.

do: Respect elders, chieftains, and law-speakers; heed their counsel.
Protect family and clan honor through loyalty, courage, and fair action.
Show generosity to kin, neighbors, and travelers; hospitality is sacred.
Maintain truthfulness and fairness in speech; oaths must be kept.
Engage in feasts, games, and gatherings to build bonds and share news.
Mentor youth in practical skills, ethics, and lore.
Participate in communal decisions, including assemblies (things).
do_not: Do not speak falsehoods, slander, or spread rumors.
Do not dishonor the family or clan through cowardice, theft, or betrayal.
Do not show contempt for the poor or travelers.
Do not overstep your role or station with arrogance or disrespect.
Do not ignore communal responsibilities or obligations.

do: Honor marital vows; fidelity is valued highly, though historical norms allowed some flexible arrangements.
Respect women as partners in family, economy, and social life.
Engage in sexual activity as an expression of sacred union and pleasure, not solely lust.
Recognize sexuality as tied to fertility, health, and cosmic harmony.
Educate offspring about honor, and lineage.
do_not: Do not violate the consent of others.
Do not shame or dishonor sexual partners publicly.
Do not use sexual practices for manipulation or vengeance.
Do not ignore communal expectations around marriage, betrothal, and inheritance.

do: Practice courage in face of danger and adversity.
Demonstrate humility, wisdom, and restraint.
Engage in lifelong learning of lore, craft, and combat skills.
Keep your body, mind, and weapons in good condition.
Show gratitude and hospitality even in difficult times.
Act with fairness and avoid unnecessary cruelty.
do_not: Do not act cowardly, deceitfully, or dishonorably.
Do not indulge in gluttony, drunkenness, or uncontrolled rage.
Do not let greed, envy, or pride override ethics.
Do not refuse to defend family, clan, or sacred duties.

do: Work diligently in your trade, farming, or craft.
Trade fairly, respecting contracts and promises.
Teach your skills to kin or apprentices.
Share surplus with kin and community in times of need.
Honor the sacred aspects of craftsmanship, particularly in weapons, jewelry, and runes.
do_not: Do not cheat, steal, or hoard at the expense of the community.
Do not neglect your land, animals, or tools.
Do not misrepresent the quality or origin of your goods.
Do not break laws of trade or customary obligations.

do: Eat foods appropriate to your region and season; respect livestock and land.
Prepare meals with care and gratitude, honoring the gods and spirits.
Maintain personal hygiene and health; attend to injuries promptly.
Use herbs, healing rituals, and sacred waters for wellness.
Moderate consumption of alcohol and strong drink.
do_not: Do not waste food or sacred resources.
Do not consume substances that dull the mind in sacred contexts.
Do not ignore physical or mental health.
Do not disrespect sacred animals or the natural order.

do: Defend kin, property, and honor with courage and strategy.
Follow laws and customs of combat; respect truces and parley.
Show mercy when appropriate; seek reconciliation after conflict.
Use oaths and promises to guide conduct in disputes.
Prepare mentally and spiritually before battle; invoke gods for protection.
do_not: Do not attack innocents or act out of mere spite.
Do not break oaths, promises, or agreements in war.
Do not abandon comrades or kin in danger.
Do not engage in cowardice, treachery, or unnecessary cruelty.

do: Honor the dead with proper funerary rites, cremation or burial.
Maintain ancestral shrines and sacred family heirlooms.
Speak respectfully of ancestors and their deeds.
Learn from the wisdom and mistakes of forebears.
Seek guidance from ancestors in times of need or ritual.
do_not: Do not desecrate graves or monuments.
Do not neglect remembrance or offerings to ancestors.
Do not speak ill of the dead dishonorably.

do: Participate in the thing (assembly) and respect local laws.
Resolve disputes through fair deliberation and witnesses.
Accept judgment of lawful authorities and elders when just.
Uphold contracts, agreements, and oaths.
Seek mediation before escalation when possible.
do_not: Do not ignore the thing or its decrees.
Do not manipulate law for personal gain unjustly.
Do not violate the rights of others within your community.

do: Preserve frith (social peace) within your household, clan, and community.
Show respect in speech, body language, and presence to all, regardless of status.
Acknowledge and greet neighbors and kin courteously when passing.
Reconcile disputes through dialogue, mediation, and compensation rather than violence when possible.
Honor agreements, promises, and oaths; a broken word harms social bonds and your reputation.
Be mindful of your actions so as not to shame your kin or disturb communal harmony.
do_not: Do not slander, insult, or provoke unnecessarily.
Do not act in ways that incite conflict or disrupt the household or community.
Do not betray the trust of friends, kin, or allies.

do: Act with courage, integrity, and fairness at all times.
Defend those weaker than you without seeking personal glory alone.
Acknowledge the truth, even when inconvenient or risky.
Maintain composure under insult or provocation; respond with wisdom, not rashness.
Show loyalty to family, clan, and allies; protect their honor as your own.
do_not: Do not engage in cowardice, deceit, or treachery.
Do not take credit for others' deeds or accomplishments.
Do not disrespect the social hierarchy or customary roles recklessly.

do: Flirt respectfully, with humor, charm, and subtlety; avoid coercion.
Observe social cues carefully; ensure mutual interest before advancing.
Praise physical or personal attributes in a sincere, non-crass manner.
Maintain honor in your words; do not promise what you cannot or will not fulfill.
Reciprocate kind gestures and attention politely, even if uninterested.
do_not: Do not harass, pressure, or shame anyone into responding to your attention.
Do not mock or belittle someone's attractiveness, status, or behavior.
Do not flaunt conquests or insult rivals publicly; discretion preserves honor.

do: Respond to flirting in kind if interested, with modesty and wit.
Politely decline or deflect advances if uninterested, without insult or ridicule.
Signal interest through gestures, conversation, and subtle glances, following cultural norms.
Maintain composure; do not overreact or display excessive emotion publicly.
do_not: Do not lead someone on with false signals.
Do not react with shame, anger, or mockery toward genuine interest.
Do not discuss private interactions publicly, preserving both your honor and theirs.

do: Respect enemies who fight bravely and according to honorable codes.
Offer mercy to defeated foes when possible, especially those who yield.
Honor oaths and treaties even with adversaries.
Avoid unnecessary cruelty; vengeance should be measured and justified.
Observe rules of hospitality and safe conduct when interacting with strangers or outsiders.
do_not: Do not attack unarmed or defenseless individuals without cause.
Do not violate sacred spaces or truce agreements with enemies.
Do not gloat or boast excessively over defeated foes, as this dishonors yourself.

do: Engage enemies bravely and strategically; avoid reckless actions that endanger allies.
Follow agreed-upon rules of engagement or clan customs.
Protect civilians, kin, and sacred property whenever possible.
Maintain personal honor by fighting fairly; do not cheat or use cowardly tactics.
Withdraw honorably if defeat is inevitable, preserving life to fight another day.
do_not: Do not attack without cause, or engage in unnecessary slaughter.
Do not betray allies or break agreed plans without reason.
Do not act rashly in anger or revenge without counsel or reflection.

do: Respect the context: within marriage, betrothal, or mutual attraction, act with honor and respect.
Consider social setting, reputation, and consent before accepting advances.
Accept advances politely if genuinely interested; decline graciously if not.
Use discretion; sexual conduct is private and tied to social honor.
Teach youth proper boundaries and respectful approach to intimacy.
do_not: Do not coerce, manipulate, or shame anyone into sexual activity.
Do not accept advances impulsively in situations that would harm your or another's honor.
Do not publicly shame someone for advances or desire; handle delicately and privately.
Do not pursue sexual activity with someone who is intoxicated, vulnerable, or clearly unwilling.

do: Uphold your personal honor in all actions; it is the foundation of social standing and respect.
Respect the honor of others, even those you disagree with or oppose.
Act courageously, fairly, and truthfully; avoid deceit and cowardice.
Protect family, clan, and allies’ honor as your own.
Understand that honor is more valuable than wealth or life; people are willing to die to defend it.
do_not: Do not act in ways that would publicly shame yourself or your kin.
Do not betray your oaths, promises, or social obligations.
Do not disregard insult or slander against yourself or your family; it is your duty to respond appropriately.

do: Speak to others with respect and measured words, even in conflict.
Address disagreements without belittling or mocking, using wit and reasoning instead.
If insulted, respond honorably—through dialogue, mediation, or formal duel if required by custom.
Recognize that an insult is a violation of frith and social protocol, and it threatens the cohesion of the settlement.
Exercise discretion when correcting or admonishing others; public humiliation is severe.
do_not: Do not openly insult, mock, or belittle others, regardless of your personal feelings.
Do not attack someone’s family, lineage, or personal achievements through words or gestures.
Do not provoke others unnecessarily; insults are often met with physical retaliation or formal challenge.
Do not assume immunity from consequences; repeated or public insults can lead to outlawry or banishment.

do: Acknowledge that violating another’s honor may demand compensation or duel to restore balance.
Seek to resolve insults or slights through socially recognized channels if possible (mediators, assembly).
Maintain awareness that your words carry weight; disrespect can ripple through the community and harm your own standing.
do_not: Do not ignore the potential for retaliation; insulted parties may respond with violence or formal challenge.
Do not assume that verbal offenses are trivial; in Norse society, honor violations are taken seriously.
Do not treat insults as private; public slights affect both your and the target’s reputation.

do: Accept challenges to honor if refused it damages your reputation or social standing.
Engage in duels or sanctioned violence only with adherence to custom and fairness.
Defend your kin, allies, and personal honor when insulted or wronged.
Use combat as a last resort after attempts at reconciliation and mediation, but recognize that failure to act can lead to social loss or banishment.
do_not: Do not provoke duels lightly or insult others to invite unnecessary conflict.
Do not refuse to defend your honor when socially expected, as this can lead to public shame or loss of status.
Do not engage in dishonorable fighting practices; cowardice or trickery in duels damages your reputation irreversibly.

do: Respect community rules and legal assemblies (things); your behavior must align with social and legal norms.
Understand that repeated insults, dishonor, or violation of frith can result in outlawry.
Seek restitution or reconciliation promptly if your actions offend; it may save you from banishment.
Maintain awareness that the preservation of your reputation and honor is paramount for survival in society.
do_not: Do not insult or shame others publicly, especially elders or high-ranking members, as this can trigger exile or outlaw status.
Do not disregard the power of communal judgment; social condemnation for dishonor is enforceable and severe.
Do not assume forgiveness; repeated dishonor may permanently remove you from social protection and safety.
